title: client_id in create-input structs is unvalidated and undocumented
description: CustomEmojiCreateInput, SpaceInviteCreateInput, SpaceBanCreateInput, and MessageCreateInput all have client_id: &str used as the JMAP create-map key. An empty string, whitespace-only string, or string containing JSON-special characters produces a malformed or ambiguous create map. No doc comment warns callers that this must be a ULID or otherwise unique non-empty string. No validation is performed at call time. The server will reject or misroute the response; the error will surface only as a SetError in not_created with no indication the client_id was the cause. Add a doc comment specifying ULID format and that empty strings are invalid.
close_reason: client_id is already documented in all create-input structs as a caller-supplied ULID; RFC 8620 does not require client-side ULID validation
